Title: Terpenoid Biosynthesis and Specialized Vascular Cells of Conifer Defense

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ethylene biosynthesis gene induced in association with traumatic resin duct formation, acting upstream of jasmonate-linked defense responses.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced and localized to resin duct epithelial cells, polyphenolic phloem parenchyma cells and ray parenchyma.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ethylene signaling is implicated upstream of octadecanoid/jasmonate signaling in traumatic resin duct formation. 2. ACC synthase transcripts and proteins are induced in Douglas fir. 3. The gene product localizes to cell types associated with resin duct defense anatomy.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review directly links ethylene biosynthesis genes with specialized defense anatomy supporting terpenoid defense.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ethylene biosynthesis gene induced in association with traumatic resin duct formation, supporting upstream signaling for terpenoid defense responses.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced and localized to resin duct epithelial cells, polyphenolic phloem parenchyma cells and ray parenchyma.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACC oxidase is induced in Douglas fir during defense-related responses. 2. The protein/transcripts localize to resin duct-related and phloem/ray parenchyma cells. 3. These data support upstream ethylene control of traumatic resin duct formation associated with terpenoid defense.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses pyruvate and D-glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate to form 1-deoxy-D-xylulose 5-phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides precursor flux for MEP-derived terpenoids; type II DXS isoforms are induced with defense-associated terpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Can play a rate-limiting role for production of MEP-derived isoprenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXS catalyzes the first step of the MEP pathway. 2. Type II DXS isoforms in conifers are stress/MeJA responsive and associated with specialized terpenoid defense metabolism. 3. Type I DXS is constitutive and linked more to primary metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; expression analysis under stress/MeJA; literature-reviewed functional assignment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: mechanical wounding; fungal treatment; methyl jasmonate; insect attack
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review-level synthesis; focal examples include PaDXS1, PaDXS2a, PaDXS2b, PdDXS1 and PdDXS2.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely supports constitutive/general isoprenoid metabolism rather than inducible oleoresin defense.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Type I DXS; does not change with MeJA treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not induced by MeJA in Norway spruce.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaDXS1 is a type I DXS isoform. 2. Its transcript levels do not change upon MeJA treatment. 3. It is therefore contrasted with inducible type II DXS genes in defense terpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis after MeJA treatment

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes inducible defense-related terpenoid precursor supply in response to MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of a DXS step considered potentially rate-limiting for MEP-derived isoprenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Type II DXS; transcript abundance increases with MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by MeJA in Norway spruce bark/stem tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaDXS2a is a type II DXS isoform linked to specialized metabolism. 2. Transcript abundance increases upon MeJA treatment. 3. It is implicated in induced oleoresin terpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis after MeJA treatment

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes inducible defense-related terpenoid precursor supply in response to MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of a DXS step considered potentially rate-limiting for MEP-derived isoprenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Type II DXS; transcript abundance increases with MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by MeJA in Norway spruce.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaDXS2b is a stress-inducible type II DXS isoform. 2. Its transcripts increase in MeJA-treated trees. 3. It is associated with induced conifer oleoresin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis after MeJA treatment

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports inducible terpenoid precursor formation; transcripts are upregulated by wounding/fungal treatment in spruce.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXR is a characterized conifer MEP-pathway gene. 2. DXR transcripts are upregulated by mechanical wounding and fungal treatment. 3. This supports a role in induced oleoresin terpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: mechanical wounding; fungal treatment; methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review-level synthesis; specific example PdDXR cloned from Pinus densiflora.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to precursor supply but was not specifically induced by wounding/MeJA unlike PdDXS2 and PdHDR2.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Most abundant in wood; no reported upregulation under wounding or MeJA in the review summary.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PdDXR was cloned from Pinus densiflora. 2. Transcript abundance is highest in wood. 3. The review specifically notes induction for PdDXS2 and PdHDR2, implying PdDXR was not notably induced by wounding/MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; tissue expression analysis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces HMBPP to form IPP and DMAPP.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports defense terpenoid precursor formation; conifer HDR genes can be induced by wounding/fungal treatment, and some isoforms respond to MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HDR is a cloned and characterized conifer MEP-pathway gene. 2. HDR transcripts are upregulated in response to mechanical wounding and fungal treatment in spruce. 3. Specific inducible isoforms such as PdHDR2 are associated with defense-related oleoresin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: mechanical wounding; fungal treatment; methyl jasmonate

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to general terpenoid precursor supply but is not highlighted as inducible by wounding/MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PdHDR1 is one of two HDR isoforms cloned from Pinus densiflora. 2. Transcript abundance is highest in wood. 3. The review highlights inducibility for PdHDR2, not PdHDR1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; tissue expression analysis

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcripts most abundant in wood and upregulated by wounding or MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in wood; upregulated in response to mechanical wounding or MeJA treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes inducible terpenoid precursor supply for defense oleoresin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PdHDR2 is an inducible HDR isoform in Pinus densiflora. 2. It is most abundant in wood. 3. It is specifically upregulated by mechanical wounding and MeJA treatment, linking it to defense terpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; tissue expression analysis; stress induction analysis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses IPP and DMAPP to form geranyl diphosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides GPP substrate for induced monoterpene biosynthesis in Norway spruce.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript levels strongly increased in response to MeJA treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaIDS1 is a homodimeric GPPS-like enzyme from Norway spruce. 2. It makes GPP as its sole product. 3. Its transcripts strongly increase upon MeJA treatment, suggesting a role in induced monoterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; enzyme product assay; expression analysis after MeJA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces substantial amounts of FPP, GGPP as well as GPP.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not implicated in induced monoterpene production in Norway spruce.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaIDS2 is similar to the Arabidopsis homodimeric GPPS-like protein. 2. It forms multiple prenyl diphosphates including FPP, GGPP and GPP. 3. Because it is not induced by MeJA, it is not considered involved in induced monoterpene production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; enzyme product assay; expression analysis

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies cytosolic FPP for sesquiterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaIDS4 is an FPPS from Norway spruce. 2. It clusters with angiosperm FPPS proteins. 3. It lacks a plastid targeting sequence and is therefore likely cytosolic.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; phylogenetic analysis; sequence-based localization inference
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named in text as PaID54, interpreted as PaIDS4 based on context.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate for diterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely plays a role in conifer oleoresin biosynthesis and defense-related diterpenoid formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcripts respond strongly to MeJA treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaIDS5 is one of two GGPPS genes cloned from Norway spruce. 2. Its transcripts respond strongly to MeJA treatment. 3. It is suggested to play a role in conifer oleoresin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; expression analysis after MeJA

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: May function in general metabolism rather than defense oleoresin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: PaIDS6 transcripts did not respond to MeJA treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaIDS6 is a Norway spruce GGPPS isoform. 2. Its transcripts are not induced by MeJA. 3. The review suggests it may function in general metabolism rather than induced oleoresin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; expression analysis

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: diverse mono-, sesqui-, and diterpene skeletons
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes or rearranges prenyl diphosphates to produce mono-, sesqui- and diterpene hydrocarbons and alcohols.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates the major chemical diversity of conifer oleoresin and is strongly induced by insect attack and MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS gene families generate much of the chemical diversity of conifer oleoresin. 2. Many conifer TPS are multi-product enzymes. 3. TPS transcripts, proteins, enzyme activities and products are strongly induced by insect attack and MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; biochemical enzyme characterization; transcriptomics; proteomics; enzyme activity assays; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: insect feeding; methyl jasmonate; mechanical wounding
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes many TPS genes across spruce, pine, fir, and Douglas-fir.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated by insect attack, contributing to induced monoterpene defense in Sitka spruce.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Gene is upregulated upon white pine weevil attack.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in Sitka spruce stems after insect feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Insect attack upregulates monoTPS genes in Sitka spruce. 2. The gene corresponding to (-)-pinene synthase is specifically induced. 3. This supports a role in induced monoterpene defense against weevil attack.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis under insect attack; metabolite accumulation context

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Substantially induced by weevil feeding but not by MeJA, suggesting a special role in insect-defense response.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Accumulated under weevil feeding; not induced by MeJA in the cited comparison.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased with weevil feeding in Sitka spruce stem tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Weevil feeding induced substantial accumulation of longifolene synthase. 2. MeJA treatment did not induce this sesquiTPS to the same extent. 3. The result suggests a specialized role of sesquiterpenes in insect defense.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative treatment expression/protein accumulation analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: white pine weevil feeding; methyl jasmonate

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by MeJA in needles with concomitant increase in volatile emissions and enzyme activities.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in needles upon MeJA treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeJA dramatically increases volatile terpene emissions from needles. 2. Transcripts corresponding to (-)-linalool synthase are induced upon MeJA treatment. 3. Increased transcripts coincide with increased enzyme activity and (-)-linalool emission.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transcript analysis; enzyme activity assay; volatile metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induced by MeJA and highlighted as having an important role in conifer defense.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript and protein both induced by MeJA in bark tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially induced in MeJA-treated Norway spruce bark.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Targeted proteomics showed differential induction of individual TPS proteins and transcripts by MeJA. 2. (+)-3-Carene synthase and (+)-3-carene were induced to a higher degree than other measured targets. 3. This highlighted an important role for (+)-3-carene in conifer defense.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: selected reaction monitoring proteomics; transcript quantification; enzyme activity; metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: various diterpene olefins; diterpene alcohols; diterpene aldehydes
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: oxidized diterpenoids including diterpene resin acid intermediates/products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes at least two of the three consecutive oxidation steps in diterpene resin acid formation from multiple diterpene substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly drives formation of diterpene resin acids that accumulate in conifer oleoresin.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP720B1/PtAO is the first functionally characterized conifer P450 in diterpene resin acid biosynthesis. 2. It catalyzes at least two of the three sequential oxidation steps leading to resin acids. 3. It is multisubstrate and multifunctional, contributing to oleoresin chemical diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization of cloned gene; enzyme assay with multiple substrates
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Member of the conifer-specific CYP720B family.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 24 different diterpenoids including olefins, alcohols and aldehydes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation of a wide range of diterpenoids across homologous series of olefins, alcohols, aldehydes and resin acids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes broad oxidation capacity for formation/diversification of diterpene resin acids in white spruce oleoresin.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A genomic clone and corresponding full-length cDNA of white spruce CYP720B4 were obtained. 2. The enzyme oxidizes 24 diterpenoids across several homologous series. 3. This broad substrate promiscuity supports major contribution to oleoresin diterpenoid diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; functional characterization enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional characterization noted as unpublished data in 2009 within the review.
